LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT FRANK MYERS' MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT FRANK MYERS CONCEDES THAT THE GROUNDS FOR RELIEF IN THE MOTION, THAT THERE WAS AN AUTOMATIC BANKRUPTCY STAY PURSUANT TO 11 U.S.C. 362, DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE "THE AUTOMATIC STAY EVAPORATED AFTER 30 DAYS." (REPLY, P. 2:11.) TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT NOW SEEKS NEW OR DIFFERENT RELIEF IN REPLY BASED ON HIS FORMER COUNSEL'S PURPORTED MISTAKE OF LAW, THESE GROUNDS DO NOT APPEAR IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION, THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THESE GROUNDS. (LURI V. GREENWALD (2003) 107 CA4TH 1119, 1124-1125.). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976 (302)

Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Default
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT FRANK MYERS' Motion To Vacate Default is Denied without prejudice. Defendant Frank Myers concedes that the grounds for relief in the motion, that there was an automatic bankruptcy stay pursuant to 11 U.S.C. 362, does not apply because "the automatic stay evaporated after 30 days." (Reply, p. 2:11.) To the extent that Defendant now seeks new or different relief in reply based on his former counsel's purported mistake of law, these grounds do not appear in the notice of motion, the Court does not consider these grounds. (Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 CA4th 1119, 1124-1125.). Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)

Order To Show Cause To Defendant As To Why The Court Should Not Grant Plaintiff'S Request For Evidentiary Sanction (Per 12/06/13 Court Order)
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Atty. Edward Higginbotham (415-581-0885) appear for plaintiff Law Offices of Edward Higginbotham moving party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Order To Show Cause To Defendant As To Why The Court Should Not Grant Plaintiff'S Request For Evidentiary Sanction (Per 12/06/13 Court Order). The Court previously ordered defendant to provide 'complete, verified Discovery responses' and awarded sanctions for defendant's prior failure to respond to discovery or to meet and confer attempts. Plaintiff then renewed its motion after receiving inadequate supplemental responses and after having provided seven extensions to the discovery response deadline. Defendant did not oppose the motion, the hearing on which was set for December 6, 2013. In its Order on December 6, 2013, the Court ruled that it had previously ruled on the relevance of the discovery responses through its prior order that defendant provide complete, verified responses, and therefore did not revisit that issue. The Court further ruled that defendant had not provided 'complete, verified' responses and had therefore not complied with the Court's prior order. The Court then granted plaintiff's motion to compel discovery response and for monetary sanctions in the amount of $4,590 and ordered defendant to appear before this Court on January 23, 2014 and to show cause as to why the Court should not grant plaintiff's request for evidentiary sanctions. The Court permitted plaintiff to submit any further briefing it wished to provide in connection with that hearing and ordered plaintiff to also submit a proposed order in advance of that hearing that identifies what liability facts alleged in plaintiff's complaint are sufficiently related to the deficient discovery responses that they should be deemed conclusively established as a further discovery sanction. Plaintiff was also ordered to submit a courtesy copy of the operative complaint. Plaintiff submitted a supplemental declaration and a proposed order. Defendant has not submitted any opposition or response to the OSC. Accordingly, the Court grants Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions for defendants' failure to comply with the Court's prior orders, failure to provide discovery responses, and failure to pay awarded sanctions. However, the Court will not award the future costs of arbitration or litigation as requested by Plaintiff's proposed order. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Geoffrey Howard, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses, Including Responses To Interrogatories, Admissions And Document Requests For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-06-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ed Higginbotham, Esq. of the Law Office of Edward M. Higginbotham, Tel. 415.581.0885, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The party stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge. The Court adopted its uncontested tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF LAW OFFICE OF EDWARD M. HIGGINBOTHAM's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Including Responses To Interrogatories, Admissions And Document Requests For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions. The Court previously ordered defendant to provide "complete, verified Discovery responses" and awarded sanctions for defendant's prior failure to respond to discovery or to meet and confer attempts. Plaintiff now renews its motion after receiving inadequate supplemental responses and after having provided seven extensions to the discovery response deadline. Defendant has not opposed the motion. The Court previously ruled on the relevance of the discovery responses through its prior order that defendant provide complete, verified responses, and therefore the Court does not revisit that issue here. Instead, the question before the Court is whether defendant has complied with the Court's prior order. The Court concludes that defendant has not provided "complete, verified" responses. Accordingly, plaintiff's motion to compel discovery response and for monetary sanctions in the amount of $4,590 is granted. Defendant is ordered to appear before this Court on January 23, 2014 and to show cause as to why the Court should not grant plaintiff's request for evidentiary sanctions. Plaintiff shall submit any further briefing it wishes to provide in connection with that hearing. Plaintiff shall also submit a proposed order in advance of that hearing that identifies what liability facts alleged in plaintiff's complaint are sufficiently related to the deficient discovery responses that they should be deemed conclusively established as a further discovery sanction. Plaintiff shall also submit a courtesy copy of the operative complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Geoffrey Howard; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
